candidates.name: Kevin Stitt
candidates.notes: One of the most overtly Christian governors in America. Publicly declared he was 'claiming Oklahoma for Jesus' at a church event (2022). Signed constitutional carry (2019). Issued executive order making Oklahoma 'strongest defender of religious liberty in the nation' (2025) — ordering state agencies to purge barriers between church and state. Backed Bible-in-schools curriculum through State Superintendent Ryan Walters and Heritage Foundation partnership. Filed amicus brief supporting St. Isidore Catholic virtual charter school at SCOTUS. Launched Office of Faith-Based Initiatives. NRA A+ rating. Signed near-total abortion ban. Cherokee Nation citizen. Term-limited in 2026.

profile.background: Businessman and mortgage company CEO (Gateway Mortgage Group); enrolled member of the Cherokee Nation; became 28th governor in 2019.

candidates.name: Matt Pinnell
candidates.notes: President of the Oklahoma Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Oklahoma_Senate.

candidates.name: James Lankford
candidates.notes: Ordained Southern Baptist minister and former director of Falls Creek Baptist Conference Center (largest Christian camp in US, 50,000+ campers annually). Led bipartisan border deal negotiations in 2024 — deal killed by Trump opposition; censured by Oklahoma GOP. Heritage Action dropped to 50% in 118th Congress from 98% in 117th due to border deal vote. Voted for Big Beautiful Bill in 2025. Strong pro-life record (FRC 100%). NRA A+ rating. Open to stronger background checks but opposes assault weapons bans. Authored federal religious liberty protections. Lost significant MAGA support over border deal but remains solidly conservative on life, marriage, religious liberty.

profile.education: University of Texas at Austin (BS Secondary Education), Southwestern Baptist Theological Seminary (MDev)
profile.background: Former youth camp director at Falls Creek Baptist Conference Center for 15 years. Former U.S. House member. Known for budget and immigration policy work.

candidates.name: Alan Armstrong
candidates.notes: Energy executive appointed to fill Markwayne Mullin's Senate seat after Mullin became DHS Secretary (March 2026). Spent nearly 4 decades at Williams Companies (major natural gas pipeline company). Gov. Stitt praised him as 'strong business leader who understands the power of free markets and limited government.' Has announced he will NOT seek election in November 2026 special election — serving as caretaker senator. No legislative voting record yet. Positions on social/cultural issues largely unknown. Strong on energy independence and free market economics based on career background.

profile.background: Energy executive who spent nearly four decades at Williams Companies, serving as CEO and executive chairman. Appointed to fill Markwayne Mullin's seat after Mullin became DHS Secretary in 2026.

candidates.name: Kyle Hilbert
candidates.notes: Speaker of the Oklahoma House — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speaker_of_the_Oklahoma_House_of_Representatives.
